sn22.126:1.2.pli: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn22.126:1.2.eng: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:

sn22.126:1.3.eng: “Sir, they speak of this thing called ‘ignorance’.

sn22.126:1.5.eng: And how is an ignorant person defined?”

sn22.126:2.1.pli: “Idha, bhikkhu, assutavā puthujjano samudayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ ‘samudayadhammaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti;
sn22.126:2.1.eng: “Mendicant, it’s when an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t truly understand form, which is liable to originate, as form which is liable to originate.

sn22.126:2.2.eng: They don’t truly understand form, which is liable to fall, as form which is liable to fall.

sn22.126:2.3.eng: They don’t truly understand form, which is liable to originate and fall, as form which is liable to originate and fall.

sn22.126:2.11.eng: consciousness, which is liable to originate, as consciousness which is liable to originate.

sn22.126:2.12.eng: They don’t truly understand consciousness, which is liable to fall, as consciousness which is liable to fall.

sn22.126:2.13.eng: They don’t truly understand consciousness, which is liable to originate and fall, as consciousness which is liable to originate and fall.

sn22.126:2.15.eng: And this is how an ignorant person is defined.”

sn22.126:3.1.pli: Evaṁ vutte, so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn22.126:3.1.eng: When he said this, the mendicant said to the Buddha:

sn22.126:3.2.eng: “Sir, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’.

sn22.126:3.4.eng: And how is a knowledgeable person defined?”

sn22.126:4.1.pli: “Idha, bhikkhu, sutavā ariyasāvako samudayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ ‘samudayadhammaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti;
sn22.126:4.1.eng: “Mendicant, it’s when a learned noble disciple truly understands form, which is liable to originate, as form which is liable to originate.

sn22.126:4.2.eng: They truly understand form, which is liable to fall, as form which is liable to fall.

sn22.126:4.3.eng: They truly understand form, which is liable to originate and vanish, as form which is liable to originate and vanish.

sn22.126:4.11.eng: consciousness, which is liable to originate, as consciousness which is liable to originate.

sn22.126:4.12.eng: They truly understand consciousness, which is liable to fall, as consciousness which is liable to fall.

sn22.126:4.13.eng: They truly understand consciousness, which is liable to originate and fall, as consciousness which is liable to originate and fall.

sn22.126:4.15.eng: And this is how a knowledgeable person is defined.”

sn22.127:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca mahākoṭṭhiko bārāṇasiyaṁ viharanti isipatane migadāye.
sn22.127:1.1.eng: At one time Venerable Sāriputta and Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita were staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana. …

sn22.127:1.2.pli: Atha kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:

sn22.127:1.3.eng: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘ignorance’.

sn22.127:1.5.eng: And how is an ignorant person defined?”

sn22.127:2.1.pli: “Idhāvuso assutavā puthujjano samudayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ ‘samudayadhammaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti; vayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ …pe… ‘samudayavayadhammaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
sn22.127:2.1.eng: “Reverend, it’s when an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t truly understand form, which is liable to originate … liable to fall … liable to originate and fall, as form which is liable to originate and fall.

sn22.127:2.2.pli: Samudayadhammaṁ vedanaṁ …pe… vayadhammaṁ vedanaṁ …pe… ‘samudayavayadhammā vedanā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.

sn22.127:2.4.pli: samudayadhamme saṅkhāre …pe… vayadhamme saṅkhāre …pe… samudayavayadhamme saṅkhāre ‘samudayavayadhammā saṅkhārā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.

sn22.127:2.5.pli: Samudayadhammaṁ viññāṇaṁ …pe… samudayavayadhammaṁ viññāṇaṁ ‘samudayavayadhammaṁ viññāṇan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
sn22.127:2.5.eng: consciousness, which is liable to originate … liable to fall … liable to originate and fall, as consciousness which is liable to originate and fall.

sn22.127:2.7.eng: And this is how an ignorant person is defined.”

sn22.128:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca mahākoṭṭhiko bārāṇasiyaṁ viharanti isipatane migadāye …pe…
sn22.128:1.1.eng: At one time Venerable Sāriputta and Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita were staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana. …

sn22.128:1.2.pli: ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:

sn22.128:1.3.eng: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’.

sn22.128:1.5.eng: And how is a knowledgeable person defined?”

sn22.128:2.1.pli: “Idhāvuso, sutavā ariyasāvako samudayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ ‘samudayadhammaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti; vayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ …pe… samudayavayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ ‘samudayavayadhammaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti;
sn22.128:2.1.eng: “Reverend, it’s when a learned noble disciple truly understands form, which is liable to originate … liable to fall … liable to originate and fall, as form which is liable to originate and fall.

sn22.128:2.4.pli: samudayadhamme saṅkhāre … vayadhamme saṅkhāre … samudayavayadhamme saṅkhāre ‘samudayavayadhammā saṅkhārā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.

sn22.128:2.5.pli: Samudayadhammaṁ viññāṇaṁ … vayadhammaṁ viññāṇaṁ … samudayavayadhammaṁ viññāṇaṁ ‘samudayavayadhammaṁ viññāṇan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
sn22.128:2.5.eng: consciousness, which is liable to originate … liable to fall … liable to originate and fall, as consciousness which is liable to originate and fall.

sn22.128:2.7.eng: And this is how a knowledgeable person is defined.”

sn22.129:1.2.pli: ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:

sn22.129:1.3.eng: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘ignorance’.

sn22.129:1.5.eng: And how is an ignorant person defined?”

sn22.129:2.1.pli: “Idhāvuso assutavā puthujjano rūpassa assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
sn22.129:2.1.eng: “Reverend, an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t truly understand the gratification, the drawback, and the escape when it comes to form,

sn22.129:2.5.pli: viññāṇassa assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.

sn22.129:2.7.eng: And this is how an ignorant person is defined.”

sn22.13:1.2.pli: “Rūpaṁ, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ, vedanā dukkhā, saññā dukkhā, saṅkhārā dukkhā, viññāṇaṁ dukkhaṁ.
sn22.13:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness are suffering.

sn22.13:1.4.eng: They understand: ‘… there is nothing further for this place.’”

sn22.130:1.2.eng: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’.

sn22.130:1.4.eng: And how is a knowledgeable person defined?”

sn22.130:2.1.pli: “Idhāvuso, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpassa assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
sn22.130:2.1.eng: “Reverend, a learned noble disciple truly understands the gratification, the drawback, and the escape when it comes to form,

sn22.130:2.5.pli: viññāṇassa assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.

sn22.130:2.7.eng: And this is how a knowledgeable person is defined.”

sn22.131:1.2.eng: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘ignorance’.

sn22.131:1.4.eng: And how is an ignorant person defined?”

sn22.131:2.1.pli: “Idhāvuso, assutavā puthujjano rūpassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
sn22.131:2.1.eng: “Reverend, an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t truly understand the origin, the disappearance, the gratification, the drawback, and the escape when it comes to form,

sn22.131:2.5.pli: viññāṇassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.

sn22.131:2.7.eng: And this is how an ignorant person is defined.”

sn22.132:1.2.pli: ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:

sn22.132:1.3.eng: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’.

sn22.132:1.5.eng: And how is a knowledgeable person defined?”

sn22.132:2.1.pli: “Idhāvuso, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
sn22.132:2.1.eng: “Reverend, a learned noble disciple truly understands the origin, the disappearance, the gratification, the drawback, and the escape when it comes to form,

sn22.132:2.5.pli: viññāṇassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.

sn22.132:2.7.eng: And this is how a knowledgeable person is defined.”

sn22.133:1.3.pli: ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:

sn22.133:1.4.eng: “Reverend Koṭṭhita, they speak of this thing called ‘ignorance’.

sn22.133:1.6.eng: And how is an ignorant person defined?”

sn22.133:2.1.pli: “Idhāvuso, assutavā puthujjano rūpassa assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
sn22.133:2.1.eng: “Reverend, an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t truly understand the gratification, the drawback, and the escape when it comes to form,

sn22.133:2.5.pli: viññāṇassa assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.

sn22.133:2.7.eng: And this is how an ignorant person is defined.”

sn22.133:3.1.pli: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:
sn22.133:3.1.eng: When he said this, Venerable Sāriputta said to him:

sn22.133:3.2.eng: “Reverend Koṭṭhita, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’.

sn22.133:3.4.eng: And how is a knowledgeable person defined?”

sn22.133:4.1.pli: “Idhāvuso, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpassa assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
sn22.133:4.1.eng: “Reverend, a learned noble disciple truly understands the gratification, the drawback, and the escape when it comes to form,

sn22.133:4.5.pli: viññāṇassa assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.

sn22.133:4.7.eng: And this is how a knowledgeable person is defined.”

sn22.134:1.2.eng: “Reverend Koṭṭhita, they speak of this thing called ‘ignorance’.

sn22.134:1.4.eng: And how is an ignorant person defined?”

sn22.134:2.1.pli: “Idhāvuso, assutavā puthujjano rūpassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
sn22.134:2.1.eng: “Reverend, an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t truly understand the origin, the disappearance, the gratification, the drawback, and the escape when it comes to form,

sn22.134:2.5.pli: viññāṇassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.

sn22.134:2.7.eng: And this is how an ignorant person is defined.”

sn22.134:3.1.pli: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:
sn22.134:3.1.eng: When he said this, Venerable Sāriputta said to him:

sn22.134:3.2.eng: “Reverend Koṭṭhita, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’.

sn22.134:3.4.eng: And how is a knowledgeable person defined?”

sn22.134:4.1.pli: “Idhāvuso, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
sn22.134:4.1.eng: “Reverend, a learned noble disciple truly understands the origin, the disappearance, the gratification, the drawback, and the escape when it comes to form,

sn22.134:4.5.pli: viññāṇassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.

sn22.134:4.7.eng: And this is how a knowledgeable person is defined.”

sn22.135:1.2.pli: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:

sn22.135:1.3.eng: “Reverend Koṭṭhita, they speak of this thing called ‘ignorance’.

sn22.135:1.5.eng: And how is an ignorant person defined?”

sn22.135:2.1.pli: “Idhāvuso, assutavā puthujjano rūpaṁ nappajānāti, rūpasamudayaṁ nappajānāti, rūpanirodhaṁ nappajānāti, rūpanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ nappajānāti.
sn22.135:2.1.eng: “Reverend, it’s when an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t understand form, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation.

sn22.135:2.5.pli: viññāṇaṁ nappajānāti, viññāṇasamudayaṁ nappajānāti, viññāṇanirodhaṁ nappajānāti, viññāṇanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ nappajānāti.
sn22.135:2.5.eng: consciousness, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation.

sn22.135:2.7.eng: And this is how an ignorant person is defined.”

sn22.135:3.1.pli: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:
sn22.135:3.1.eng: When he said this, Venerable Sāriputta said to him:

sn22.135:3.2.eng: “Reverend Koṭṭhita, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’.

sn22.135:3.4.eng: And how is a knowledgeable person defined?”

sn22.135:4.1.pli: “Idhāvuso, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpaṁ pajānāti, rūpasamudayaṁ pajānāti, rūpanirodhaṁ pajānāti, rūpanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ pajānāti.
sn22.135:4.1.eng: “Reverend, it’s when a learned noble disciple understands form, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation.

sn22.135:4.5.pli: viññāṇaṁ pajānāti, viññāṇasamudayaṁ pajānāti, viññāṇanirodhaṁ pajānāti, viññāṇanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ pajānāti.
sn22.135:4.5.eng: consciousness, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation.

sn22.135:4.7.eng: And this is how a knowledgeable person is defined.”

sn22.136:1.2.pli: “Rūpaṁ, bhikkhave, kukkuḷaṁ, vedanā kukkuḷā, saññā kukkuḷā, saṅkhārā kukkuḷā, viññāṇaṁ kukkuḷaṁ.
sn22.136:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness are burning chaff.
sn22.136:1.2.comm: “Burning chaff” (kukkūla) is the name of a hell (MN 130:21.1). Some later Buddhists argued that this description meant that all conditioned things were nothing but scorching pain with no pleasure at all, against which the Theravadins cited a series of Sutta texts (Kv 2.8). Compare SN 22.60.

sn22.136:1.3.pli: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpasmimpi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati, saññāyapi nibbindati, saṅkhāresupi nibbindati, viññāṇasmimpi nibbindati.
sn22.136:1.3.eng: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.

sn22.136:1.4.pli: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati. Vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
sn22.136:1.4.eng: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.

sn22.136:1.5.pli: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.
sn22.136:1.5.eng: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”

sn22.137:1.2.pli: “Yaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ; tatra vo chando pahātabbo.
sn22.137:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, you should give up desire for what is impermanent.

sn22.137:1.4.pli: Rūpaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ; tatra vo chando pahātabbo.
sn22.137:1.4.eng: Form is impermanent; you should give up desire for it.

sn22.137:1.8.pli: viññāṇaṁ aniccaṁ; tatra vo chando pahātabbo.
sn22.137:1.8.eng: Consciousness is impermanent; you should give up desire for it.

sn22.137:1.9.pli: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ; tatra vo chando pahātabbo”ti.
sn22.137:1.9.eng: You should give up desire for what is impermanent.”

sn22.138:1.2.pli: “Yaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ; tatra vo rāgo pahātabbo.
sn22.138:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, you should give up greed for what is impermanent.

sn22.138:1.4.pli: Rūpaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ; tatra vo rāgo pahātabbo.
sn22.138:1.4.eng: Form is impermanent; you should give up greed for it.

sn22.138:1.8.pli: viññāṇaṁ aniccaṁ; tatra vo rāgo pahātabbo.
sn22.138:1.8.eng: Consciousness is impermanent; you should give up greed for it.

sn22.138:1.9.pli: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ; tatra vo rāgo pahātabbo”ti.
sn22.138:1.9.eng: You should give up greed for what is impermanent.”

sn22.139:1.2.pli: “Yaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ; tatra vo chandarāgo pahātabbo.
sn22.139:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, you should give up desire and greed for what is impermanent.

sn22.139:1.4.pli: Rūpaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ, tatra vo chandarāgo pahātabbo.
sn22.139:1.4.eng: Form is impermanent; you should give up desire and greed for it.

sn22.139:1.8.pli: viññāṇaṁ aniccaṁ; tatra vo chandarāgo pahātabbo.
sn22.139:1.8.eng: Consciousness is impermanent; you should give up desire and greed for it.

sn22.139:1.9.pli: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, aniccaṁ; tatra vo chandarāgo pahātabbo”ti.
sn22.139:1.9.eng: You should give up desire and greed for what is impermanent.”

sn22.14:1.2.pli: “Rūpaṁ, bhikkhave, anattā, vedanā anattā, saññā anattā, saṅkhārā anattā, viññāṇaṁ anattā.
sn22.14:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness are not-self.

sn22.14:1.3.pli: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpasmimpi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati, saññāyapi nibbindati, saṅkhāresupi nibbindati, viññāṇasmimpi nibbindati.
sn22.14:1.3.eng: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.

sn22.14:1.4.pli: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati. Vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
sn22.14:1.4.eng: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.

sn22.14:1.5.pli: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.
sn22.14:1.5.eng: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”

sn22.140:1.2.pli: “Yaṁ, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ; tatra vo chando pahātabbo …pe…
sn22.140:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, you should give up desire for what is suffering. …”

sn22.140:1.3.pli: yaṁ, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ; tatra vo chando pahātabbo”ti.

sn22.141:1.2.pli: “Yaṁ, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ; tatra vo rāgo pahātabbo …pe…
sn22.141:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, you should give up greed for what is suffering. …”

sn22.141:1.3.pli: yaṁ, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ; tatra vo rāgo pahātabbo”ti.

sn22.142:1.2.pli: “Yaṁ, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ; tatra vo chandarāgo pahātabbo …pe…
sn22.142:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, you should give up desire and greed for what is suffering. …”

sn22.142:1.3.pli: yaṁ, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ; tatra vo chandarāgo pahātabbo”ti.

sn22.143:1.2.pli: “Yo, bhikkhave, anattā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo.
sn22.143:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, you should give up desire for what is not-self. …”

sn22.143:1.4.pli: Rūpaṁ, bhikkhave, anattā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo.

sn22.143:1.8.pli: viññāṇaṁ anattā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo.

sn22.143:1.9.pli: Yo, bhikkhave, anattā; tatra vo chando pahātabbo”ti.

sn22.144:1.2.pli: “Yo, bhikkhave, anattā; tatra vo rāgo pahātabbo.
sn22.144:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, you should give up greed for what is not-self. …”

sn22.144:1.4.pli: Rūpaṁ, bhikkhave, anattā; tatra vo rāgo pahātabbo.

sn22.144:1.8.pli: viññāṇaṁ anattā; tatra vo rāgo pahātabbo.

sn22.144:1.9.pli: Yo, bhikkhave, anattā; tatra vo rāgo pahātabbo”ti.
